nativeRange.habitat: Shallow intertidal and subtidal rocky and reef substrates in warm tropical seas

decorPreferences: anchored to rockwork in moderate flow

avoidWith: aggressive herbivorous fish that may heavily graze it

imageCandidates.notes: Photo by CK2AZ (via GBIF/iNaturalist), 1536x2048. Two red-brown clumps on plain sand; sharpest clean single-subject shot showing the reddish coloration, dense fuzzy filaments and antler-like branching. Only washed-up/intertidal material exists for this rare species.

Photo by Golfopolikayak (via GBIF/iNaturalist), 1000x750. Whole fresh specimen on white background with scale ruler; clearest complete view of the antler branching form and fuzzy filament-covered branches (out-of-water tan-brown colour).

Photo by Christian Berg (via GBIF/iNaturalist), 2048x1536. In-habitat tidepool shot, sharp, showing fuzzy branched clumps in natural setting (olive/yellow high-light coloration typical of intertidal individuals).

summary: A rare, slow-growing red macroalga with fine filaments covering its branches for a distinctively fuzzy appearance. Seldom seen in the hobby; best suited to mature display reef tanks.
careNotes:
Fuzzy Antler is a bushy red macroalga belonging to the family Rhodomelaceae. Its branches are densely covered with fine filaments, producing the characteristic fuzzy, antler-like texture that gives the species its trade name. The scientific name is Digenea simplex, and the species is widely distributed across tropical and subtropical seas — including the Mediterranean, tropical Atlantic, Caribbean, and Indo-Pacific — where it grows attached to hard substrates in shallow, well-lit water.

The species is historically notable as the natural source of kainic acid, a compound traditionally used as an antiparasitic treatment in East Asian herbal medicine and later studied extensively in neuroscience.

Digenea simplex is rarely available in the aquarium hobby, occasionally offered by specialty macroalgae retailers. Growth form is upright and bushy, anchored to rockwork rather than planted in substrate. Medium lighting is generally advised for ornamental Rhodomelaceae, and moderate to strong water flow tends to be beneficial for gas exchange. Nutrient uptake is considered low relative to refugium macroalgae such as Chaetomorpha, so this species is better suited to display placement than to active nutrient export.

**MELT WARNING:** Like other red macroalgae, Digenea simplex will deteriorate at the base if buried in sand. Attach it to rockwork instead, using aquarium-safe superglue, fishing line, zip-ties, or by wedging it firmly into a crevice. No allelopathic concerns have been documented for this genus.
breedingNotes: In captivity, Fuzzy Antler is propagated by fragmentation — dividing healthy thalli and attaching the pieces to new anchor points on rockwork. In the wild, Digenea simplex follows the typical Rhodomelaceae life cycle, alternating between a gametophyte and a tetrasporophyte generation (two distinct reproductive forms in the same species' life cycle), but this complex sexual cycle has not been documented as a practical propagation method in the aquarium hobby.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby source. Product page confirms scientific name Digenea simplex, bushy form with fine filaments, very slow growth, and rarity in the hobby. No numeric water parameters provided.

additional.name: NCBI Taxonomy
additional.notes: Confirms taxonomy: Kingdom Eukaryota, Phylum Rhodophyta, Class Florideophyceae, Order Ceramiales, Family Rhodomelaceae. Basionym Conferva simplex Wulfen 1803, reclassified by C. Agardh 1822.

additional.name: Macroalgae Herbarium Portal
additional.notes: Confirms Family Rhodomelaceae. Notes synonyms including Caulerpa lycopodium, Ceramium digenea, Conferva simplex, Digenea lycopodium. Herbarium specimens from broad geographic range confirming wide tropical/subtropical distribution.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
